spt_programs.progressionJustification: DUP rotates heavy (3–5 reps), moderate (6–8 reps), and volume (10–12 reps) sessions across the week, providing sufficient mechanical loading stimulus for bone density while varying metabolic stress to support muscle retention — both critical for post-menopausal women. The within-week intensity variation also reduces joint fatigue compared to linear progression, making it ideal for a trainee starting a new program with no established maxes.
spt_programs.weeklyVolumeNotes: GLUTES_HAMSTRINGS receives 11 sets/week spread across all 3 sessions via hip thrusts, RDLs, and deadlift primary work. UPPER_BACK_ERECTORS receives 9 sets/week via rows across all 3 sessions. QUAD_DOMINANT receives 9 sets/week split across Sessions 1 and 2 primarily with goblet squat, back squat. PUSH receives 7 sets/week across Sessions 2 and 3. VERTICAL_PULL receives 7 sets/week across Sessions 1 and 3 primarily. UNILATERAL_LOWER receives 6 sets/week per leg across all 3 sessions. CORE receives 5 sets/week across all 3 sessions (accessory tier, last in session). CARRIES_LOADED appear at least once per week in strength or metcon.

sessions.label: Workout 1 for the week starting June 28, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Posterior Chain — Heavy Day (DUP: 3–5 reps)

warmup.name: Rowing Machine Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Build from very easy to moderate; focus on hip hinge drive, keep lumbar neutral

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
warmup.notes: Sit on floor, rotate between both hip positions; breathe into tightness

warmup.name: Cat-Cow on Hands and Knees
warmup.notes: Slow, controlled; emphasise thoracic extension on the 'cow' phase

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Light band; retract scapulae fully — warm-up only, not a working set

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (elbow at side)
warmup.notes: Light resistance; prime rotator cuff before any loading

warmup.name: Glute Bridge Bodyweight
warmup.notes: Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top, hold 1 s; prime posterior chain

warmup.name: Bodyweight Good Morning
warmup.notes: Hinge practice — soft knees, flat back, feel hamstring stretch

strength.coachingNotes: No established max — start with an empty bar and add weight each warm-up set (20 kg → 40 kg → 55 kg → work sets). Work sets should feel heavy but crisp: RPE 7–8. Brace hard through the belly, drive the floor away. Focus on bar path staying close to the shins. Suggested starting work weight: 55–65 kg for all 4 sets.

Use a barbell pad. Upper back on bench at shoulder-blade level. Drive hips up until torso is horizontal, squeeze glutes hard at top for 1 s. Suggested starting weight: 40–50 kg on the bar. Pair with the lat pulldown below (superset group A) to save time.

Wide grip, slight lean back, pull the bar to upper chest. Focus on depressing the shoulder blades before pulling — this directly addresses lat tightness. Suggested starting weight: 30–40 kg cable stack. Perform immediately after hip thrust; rest 120 s then repeat.

Rear foot elevated on bench, front foot forward enough so knee stays behind toes. Use light DBs to start — RPE 7, approximately 8–12 kg per hand. Complete all reps on one leg then switch. Week 1–2: establish weight at RPE 7. Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep per set at same weight.

Brace knee and same-side hand on bench. Pull DB to hip, not shoulder. Full range — let shoulder blade slide out at bottom, fully retract at top. Approximately 14–18 kg DB, RPE 7–8. Pair with split squat (superset group B): do a set of split squats, immediately do a set of rows, rest 90 s.

Lie on back, arms vertical, knees bent 90°. Press lower back firmly into floor (this is the cue). Slowly lower opposite arm and leg until they nearly touch the floor. Return. Keep breathing. Bodyweight only. This is anti-extension core work — important for spinal stability given deadlift focus today.

metcon.name: Posterior Pulse
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP. Complete as many rounds as possible of the following sequence. Move at a sustainable pace — this is not a sprint; keep technique clean on KB swings. Scale the air bike calories down if needed. Step-ups should be on a 20-inch box or lower.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Hip hinge drive, not a squat. Hike the bell back aggressively, snap hips at top. Stop at shoulder height — American swing NOT required.

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Rings set so body is at 45° when arms are extended. Keep body plank-rigid; pull chest to rings. Adjust foot position to scale difficulty.

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Unweighted or light DB in each hand (optional). Step fully onto box, drive through heel, stand tall at top. Alternate legs or do all reps on one side then switch.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Moderate effort — conversational pace. Use both arms and legs.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch (or rig assisted)
mobility.notes: Grab rig upright at hip height, step back and push hips away. Feel stretch in lat. Breathe deeply. Addresses lat tightness directly.

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: Place roller perpendicular to spine at mid-back. Support head. Gently extend over roller; move 2–3 segments. Do not force — gentle gravity stretch.

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch (shoulder IR)
mobility.notes: Side-lying with shoulder at 90°, push wrist gently toward floor. Hold at first point of resistance. Critical for limited internal rotation.

mobility.name: Figure-4 Hip Stretch (Piriformis)
mobility.notes: Lie on back, cross ankle over opposite knee, pull thigh toward chest. Complements posterior chain work done today.

sessions.label: Workout 2 for the week starting June 28, 2026

warmup.name: Air Bike Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Low effort warm-up; get the joints moving before any loading

warmup.name: Thoracic Rotation in 90/90 Position
warmup.notes: Sit in 90/90 hip position, hands behind head, rotate upper torso. Emphasise reaching elbow to ceiling.

warmup.name: Band Y/T/W Raises (prone or standing lean)
warmup.notes: Light band or bodyweight. Y = arms overhead; T = arms out wide; W = pull elbows back. Warm-up only.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Light band; focus on squeezing shoulder blades together at full extension

warmup.name: Goblet Squat Bodyweight (slow descent)
warmup.notes: Hold a 12 kg KB to chest. 4-second descent, pause 2 s at bottom, drive up. Primes quads and teaches bracing.

warmup.name: Wrist Circles + Wrist Extension Stretch
warmup.notes: Rotate wrists slowly in each direction, then press palm against wall for flexor stretch — mandatory wrist/forearm prep element

strength.coachingNotes: No established max — begin with bar only, add 10–15 kg each warm-up set, find a moderate working weight where rep 6 is RPE 7–8. Suggested starting work weight: 45–55 kg. Bar high-bar position. Descend until thighs are parallel — depth before load. Brace and breathe with each rep.

Set barbell in landmine attachment or corner. Standing position, grip with both hands, press at ~45° angle. This angle is far safer for the shoulder than a vertical press — preferred for limited internal rotation. Start with 10–15 kg on the sleeve. Superset with barbell row (group A): press, immediately row, then rest 120 s.

Hinge to roughly 45°, bar hangs at mid-shin. Pull to lower sternum, elbows track back close to ribs. Control the descent. Suggested starting weight: 35–45 kg. Pair with landmine press (superset A). This is not a hinge for volume-audit purposes — it targets upper back.
Same set-up as Session 1. Moderate weight — RPE 7, approximately 40–50 kg. Focus on full glute squeeze at top. Weeks 1–2: establish weight. Weeks 3–4: add 1 rep (11 reps) at same load.

Step back, lower rear knee to just above the floor, drive through front heel to return. Light DBs to start — approximately 8–12 kg per hand, RPE 7. Good single-leg loading without the joint stress of a forward lunge. Pair with hip thrust (group B): hip thrust set → reverse lunge set → rest 90 s.

Stand perpendicular to anchor, feet shoulder-width. Press handle straight out from chest and hold for 2 s, return. The resistance pulls you into rotation — resist it. Light band or cable, RPE 6–7. Anti-rotation core work placed last.

metcon.name: Squatman's Holiday
metcon.description: For Time — complete 4 rounds of the following. Target time 12–15 minutes. Scale goblet squat load as needed. Med ball slams are low-impact plyometric — let the ball do the work on the way down, not your lumbar. Rest as needed to maintain form.

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Hold KB at chest, elbows inside knees at bottom. Full depth. Controlled descent.

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Hands slightly wider than shoulder width. Chest touches floor. Scale to knee push-up or elevated hands on box if needed.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: Damper 4–6. Drive with legs first, then pull arms. Relax grip slightly on the recovery.

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Raise ball overhead, slam straight down. Step back to pick up. No jumping. Low-impact plyometric — power from the lats and core, not the lumbar.

mobility.name: Child's Pose with Lat Reach
mobility.notes: From child's pose, walk hands to one side and reach through the underarm. Breathe into the lat stretch. Direct treatment for lat tightness.

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: As per Session 1. Different thoracic segments — focus mid-upper back today.

mobility.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
mobility.notes: Draw arm across chest, apply gentle pressure with opposite arm at the elbow. Targets posterior capsule and assists shoulder IR.

mobility.name: Hip Flexor Kneeling Stretch
mobility.notes: Lunge position, rear knee on floor, shift hips forward. Squeeze rear glute to increase stretch. Post-squat essential.

sessions.label: Workout 3 for the week starting June 28, 2026

warmup.name: Ski Erg Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Arms only; focus on lat engagement and shoulder depression through the stroke

warmup.name: Thoracic Rotation (seated on box)
warmup.notes: Sit tall on box, arms crossed over chest, rotate thoracic without moving hips

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (lying on side)
warmup.notes: Elbow at 90°, rotate forearm toward ceiling. Rotator cuff activation before vertical pull work.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: As previous sessions — warm-up only.

warmup.name: Scapular Pull-Up or Dead Hang Shrug
warmup.notes: Hang from bar, depress and retract shoulder blades without bending elbows. Activates lower traps for pulling work.

warmup.name: Single-Leg Balance Stance
warmup.notes: Eyes open, on flat floor. Progress to eyes closed if comfortable — balance/proprioception mandatory element.

strength.coachingNotes: Volume day — lighter load, higher reps. Hinge pattern: soft knees, bar travels close to thighs, feel the hamstring stretch at the bottom (just below the knee). Do NOT round the lower back. Suggested starting weight: 45–55 kg. This is session 3 and the primary is a hinge — differs from Session 2 (squat primary) and Session 1 (hinge primary) but Session 1 was heavy and this is volume, so the pattern is justified by DUP structure.

Use a resistance band looped over the bar if needed (start with a thick band). Full hang at bottom, chin clears bar at top. Initiate by pulling shoulder blades down and together before bending elbows — directly addresses lat tightness. Superset with chest-supported row (group A).

Set bench to 30–45° incline, lie face down, row DBs to hips. Chest support eliminates lower back fatigue — good choice on a day already containing an RDL primary. Suggested starting weight: 14–18 kg per DB. Superset with pull-up.

Box height: knee height or lower. Hold light DBs at sides — approximately 8–12 kg per hand, RPE 7. Drive through the heel of the top foot, don't push off the bottom foot. Great unilateral load for bone density. Weeks 1–2 establish weight; Weeks 3–4 add 1 rep per set.

Hold a KB in each hand — approximately 16–20 kg per hand to start, RPE 7. Tall posture, shoulders packed down, core braced. Walk 30 m (or down and back in the gym). This is a grip, core, and trap stimulus and counts as the mandatory loaded carry for the week. Pair with step-up (group B).

Lie on back, press lower back into floor, raise shoulders and legs to form a 'dish' shape. Arms extended overhead. If this is too intense, bend knees to 90°. Hold for 30 s. Bodyweight only. Anti-extension core work.

metcon.name: Ski & Swing
metcon.description: 12-minute EMOM — every minute on the minute, alternate between two movements. Odd minutes: ski erg calories. Even minutes: KB deadlift + hanging knee raise. Rest for any remaining time in the minute. Scale calories and reps to finish with 15–20 s rest each minute.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Odd minutes: 10 calories on air bike. All-out effort then rest remaining time in minute. Alternates with ski erg across weeks.

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Even minutes: 8 reps DB floor press. Lie on floor, elbows at 45°, lower until triceps touch floor, press. Floor stops excessive range of motion — shoulder safe.

movements.name: hanging knee raise
movements.notes: From bar hang, pull knees to chest slowly. Control the descent. If bar is inaccessible, substitute lying leg raise.

movements.name: KB deadlift
movements.notes: Place two KBs outside feet, hinge and grip handles, drive through floor. Touch-and-go or reset each rep. Lumbar-flagged movement — only one per this metcon.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: As Session 1 — frequency important for addressing chronic lat tightness.

mobility.name: Book Opening (Thoracic Rotation, Side-Lying)
mobility.notes: Lie on side, knees stacked, top arm rotates overhead and back. Follow hand with eyes. Excellent thoracic mobility drill — different stimulus to the foam roller work.

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: As Session 1. End each week here — consistency builds lasting shoulder IR improvement.

mobility.name: Calf and Ankle Circles
mobility.notes: After step-up work — maintain ankle mobility to protect the knee during unilateral movements.

sessions.label: Workout 4 for the week starting July 5, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Posterior Chain — Volume Day (DUP: 10–12 reps)

warmup.name: Rowing Machine Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Settle into a rhythm; focus on leg drive

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
warmup.notes: As Week 1 Session 1

warmup.name: Quadruped Thoracic Rotation
warmup.notes: On hands and knees, place one hand behind head, rotate elbow toward ceiling. New variation for thoracic mobility.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (elbow at side)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff warm-up

warmup.name: Romanian Deadlift (barbell, light)
warmup.notes: Movement prep for today's volume deadlift work. Keep it light — just groove the pattern.

strength.coachingNotes: Volume day — same movement as Session 1 but lighter, more reps. Drop 15–20 kg from your Session 1 weight. Suggested work weight: 40–50 kg. Focus on smooth, controlled technique across all 10 reps. Hinge pattern repeated from Session 1 but DUP intensity changes the stimulus. Session 2 will be squat-led, maintaining pattern alternation across the week.
Moderate-heavy for glute hypertrophy. Use same or slightly more than Session 2 Week 1. Approximately 42–52 kg. Squeeze at top for 1 s each rep. Superset with lat pulldown (group A).
Same set-up as Session 1. Aim for 2 extra reps vs Session 1 (if using same weight) or same reps with slightly more weight — RPE 7–8. Approximately 30–40 kg. Superset with hip thrust.
Same as Session 2 Week 1 — use same weight and aim for cleaner reps. Approximately 8–12 kg per DB, RPE 7. Pair with single-arm row (group B).
Same as Session 1 Week 1 — match or slightly exceed that weight. Approximately 14–18 kg DB, RPE 7–8. Alternate sides within the set (12 left, 12 right = 1 set). Pair with reverse lunge (group B).
Same as Session 1. Try to slow the movement further — 3 s lowering phase. Bodyweight only. Anti-extension core placed last.

metcon.name: Nordic Pull
metcon.description: 5 Rounds For Time. Target: complete all 5 rounds in 14–16 minutes. Scale pull-up with a thick resistance band. Lunge steps should be controlled — no bouncing at the bottom. Rest briefly between rounds if needed to maintain form on pull-ups.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Hip snap, not squat. Bell to shoulder height. Lumbar-flagged — only lumbar movement in this metcon.

movements.name: pull-up
movements.notes: Strict or banded. Full hang at bottom, chin over bar at top. Scale: ring row if pull-ups are not yet accessible.

movements.name: lunge
movements.notes: Unweighted. Step forward, lower rear knee close to floor, push back to standing. Alternate legs.

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: 12 calories each round. Moderate pace — pull to hips, not just chest. Rotate away from row machine.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Consistency — every session

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: Focus upper thoracic today (near shoulder blades)

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR — maintain each session

mobility.name: Seated Piriformis Stretch (figure-4 in chair)
mobility.notes: After glute-heavy day. Cross ankle over knee, lean forward slightly.

sessions.label: Workout 5 for the week starting July 5, 2026

warmup.name: Air Bike Easy-Moderate Pace
warmup.notes: Slightly more effort than warm-up pace — raise heart rate before heavy squats

warmup.name: Ankle Mobility Drill (wall knee drive)
warmup.notes: Hands on wall, drive knee over little toe. Improves squat depth.

warmup.name: Thoracic Extension with Arms Overhead (rib cage reach)
warmup.notes: Stand, clasp hands, raise arms overhead, extend through mid-back. New warm-up variation.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (standing)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff — warm-up only

warmup.name: Back Squat (bar only and build up)
warmup.reps: 5 reps at bar, 5 at 30 kg, 3 at 40 kg
warmup.notes: Progressive warm-up sets. Focus on bracing and bar placement before working sets.

strength.coachingNotes: Heavy day — add 2.5–5 kg over Session 2 Week 1 if those sets felt RPE 7. Suggested work weight: 47.5–57.5 kg. 5 sets × 4 reps. This is a near-max effort — full rest between sets. Do NOT superset this primary. Shoes with a heel lift or squat shoes preferred.
Add 2.5 kg to sleeve vs Session 2 Week 1. Suggested: 12.5–17.5 kg on sleeve. 4 sets × 6 reps. Heavy-ish for the push pattern. Pair with DB row (group A): press → row → rest 120 s.
Week 2 — try to add 2 kg per DB or 1 extra rep vs Session 3 Week 1. Approximately 16–20 kg per DB. Pull to hips, elbows track back. Pair with landmine press (group A).
Match Session 1 Week 1 weight — approximately 8–12 kg per DB, RPE 7. Consistency and technique first in week 2. Pair with hip thrust (group B).
Third hip thrust session this week — lighter, higher reps for volume accumulation. Approximately 35–45 kg, RPE 6–7. This is accessory volume, not a primary stimulus. Pair with split squat (group B).
As Session 2 Week 1 — same set-up. Try to increase hold time at the extended position to 3 s this week. Light band or cable, RPE 6–7.

metcon.name: Tabata Ladder
metcon.description: Tabata protocol: 8 rounds of each movement (20 s work / 10 s rest). Complete all 8 rounds of Movement 1, then rest 1 min, then all 8 rounds of Movement 2, etc. Total 4 movements × 4 min each = 16 min including inter-movement rest. Count total reps for each movement and log them.

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Alternating legs. Box height: knee level. Drive through heel of top foot. Low-impact plyometric element.

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Scale to elevated hands if needed. Aim for the same reps each round — don't blow out round 1.

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Keep hips up, pull chest to rings. Adjust foot position to manage difficulty.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Max calories in 20 s. All-out effort each interval. Note calories per round.

mobility.name: Child's Pose with Lat Reach
mobility.notes: Post-squat day — decompress the spine and stretch the lats simultaneously

mobility.name: Book Opening (Side-Lying Thoracic Rotation)
mobility.notes: New variation from Week 1 Session 3 — thoracic rotation

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR maintenance

mobility.name: Quad Stretch (standing)
mobility.notes: After heavy squat day — pull heel to glute, brace wall for balance if needed

sessions.label: Workout 6 for the week starting July 5, 2026

warmup.name: Ski Erg Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Light pull — activate lats before vertical pulling work

warmup.name: Prone Y/T/W Raises (on bench)
warmup.notes: Lie face down on incline bench, perform Y, T, W raise patterns. Rotator cuff and lower trap — warm-up only.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Scapular Pull-Up Dead Hang
warmup.notes: Hang, retract and depress scapulae only. Prepare shoulders for pull-ups.

warmup.name: Rice Bucket Drill or Reverse Wrist Curls
warmup.notes: Mandatory wrist/forearm element. If no rice bucket, use a light DB for reverse wrist curls — 3 kg, slow controlled.

warmup.name: Single-Leg Balance with Perturbation
warmup.notes: Balance on one leg while a partner lightly taps your shoulders in various directions (or self-apply by catching a light ball). Balance/proprioception mandatory element.

strength.coachingNotes: Moderate day — add 2.5–5 kg vs Session 3 Week 1. Suggested: 47.5–57.5 kg. 3 sets × 8 reps. This is DUP moderate intensity for the hinge. Control the descent over 3 seconds. Session leads with hinge following last session's squat-lead — pattern alternation maintained.
Week 2 — aim for 2 more reps or thinner band vs Session 3 Week 1. 4 × 8 reps. Initiate with shoulder blades, then elbows. Superset with barbell row (group A).
Add 2.5 kg vs Session 2 Week 1. Suggested: 37.5–47.5 kg. Pair with pull-up (group A). Same technique cues: pull to lower sternum, elbows close.
Match Session 3 Week 1 — approximately 8–12 kg per DB, RPE 7. Focus on slow, controlled step down (eccentric). Pair with farmer carry (group B).
Match or slightly exceed Session 3 Week 1 — approximately 16–20 kg per hand. Maintain tall posture, don't let shoulders shrug. Mandatory loaded carry for the week. Pair with step-up (group B).
Extend the hold by 5 s vs Session 3 Week 1. If the position breaks, rest and reset rather than compensating. Bodyweight only.

metcon.name: DB Snatch Grinder
metcon.description: 12-minute AMRAP. Keep pace steady — this AMRAP has a DB snatch which requires technique focus. Rest briefly between movements rather than rushing and breaking form. Scale snatch load to a weight you can control overhead safely.

movements.name: DB snatch
movements.notes: Unilateral, overhead — counts as the sole overhead movement this session. Start light; hinge, pull, punch. Land with stable locked-out elbow. Scale load aggressively — technique is paramount. Skill-flagged: coach cues and reduce load before speed.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: 10 calories per round. Rotate away from ski erg this session.

movements.name: burpee (no-jump)
movements.notes: No jumping. Step feet back, lower chest to floor, press up, step feet in, stand. Lumbar-flagged — only other flagged movement; no ballistic hinge in this metcon so the pairing is safe.

movements.name: sit-up
movements.notes: Abmat or standard. Hands cross over chest or touch temples. Full range, controlled descent.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Every session — building a habit

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: Mid-back focus. Three positions: lower, middle, upper thoracic.

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent shoulder IR work

mobility.name: Wrist Flexor Stretch (wall)
mobility.notes: Press palm into wall with arm extended. After grip-intensive session. Mandatory wrist/forearm element.

sessions.label: Workout 7 for the week starting July 12, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Posterior Chain — Moderate Day (DUP: 6–8 reps)

warmup.name: Rowing Machine Easy-Moderate Pace
warmup.notes: Build pace over 4 minutes; warm up the posterior chain with each stroke

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 with Active Rotation
warmup.notes: Actively rotate between positions; don't just sit passively

warmup.name: Cat-Cow with Thoracic Emphasis
warmup.notes: Slow; pause in 'cow' (extension) for 2 s, really open the chest

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (elbow at side)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff warm-up

warmup.name: Goblet Squat with Heel Elevation (if needed)
warmup.notes: Warm up quads and hips; optional heel elevation to increase depth

strength.coachingNotes: Moderate day (Week 3). Add 2.5–5 kg vs the heavy day (Session 1) but do 6 reps rather than 4. Suggested: 57.5–67.5 kg. The DUP wave: Heavy (W1S1 4 reps) → Volume (W2S4 10 reps) → Moderate (W3S7 6 reps) — each week rotates differently across the three intensities. Near-moderate intensity, not a max effort — no superset needed but takes full rest.
Week 3 — add 2.5 kg vs Week 1 hip thrust, or add 1 rep per set. Approximately 42.5–52.5 kg. 4 × 8. Superset with lat pulldown (group A).
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (9 reps vs 8 last time) at same weight. Approximately 30–40 kg. Per accessory rule: Weeks 3–4 add 1 rep, same weight. Superset with hip thrust (group A).
Week 3 progression: add 1 rep per set (13 vs 12 in Weeks 1–2) at same weight — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Pair with single-arm row (group B).
Week 3 progression: 13 reps vs 12 in Weeks 1–2, same weight — approximately 14–18 kg DB. Pair with split squat (group B).
Week 3 progression: 11 reps per side vs 10 in Weeks 1–2. Same bodyweight. Focus on breath control — exhale as the limb lowers.

metcon.name: Triple Threat
metcon.description: For Time — 3 rounds of the following. Target 13–15 minutes. Single-arm row in the metcon uses a DB — different implement and context vs strength single-arm row. Med ball slams are explosive — the low-impact plyometric element for this block. Rest briefly between rounds if form degrades.

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Higher reps than Week 1 metcon. Maintain upright torso, full depth.

movements.name: single-arm row
movements.notes: Use a bench for support. Pull to hip. Both arms = one set of the movement.

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Raise overhead, slam down hard. Step to pick up — no jumping. Low-impact plyometric element.

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: 15 calories per round. Rotating from row machine last session.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent — every session

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: Week 3 — spend more time on each segment; breathe slowly

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Shoulder IR — daily if possible at home

mobility.name: Supine Hip Flexor Stretch (knee to chest / opposite leg flat)
mobility.notes: After hinge-heavy session

sessions.label: Workout 8 for the week starting July 12, 2026

warmup.name: Air Bike Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Light warm-up; think about posture and breathing

warmup.name: World's Greatest Stretch
warmup.notes: Step forward into lunge, plant same-side hand, rotate upper arm toward ceiling. Thoracic + hip in one drill.

warmup.name: Band Y/T/W Raises
warmup.notes: Scapular and rotator cuff — warm-up only

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Goblet Squat (slow tempo)
warmup.notes: 4 s down, 2 s hold, 2 s up. Build tissue tolerance for volume squats.

warmup.name: Wrist Circles + Finger Flexor Stretch
warmup.notes: Wrist/forearm mandatory element — rotate then press fingers back gently.

strength.coachingNotes: Volume day — lighter load, highest rep count. Drop approximately 15 kg from your heavy-day working weight. Suggested: 32–42 kg. Focus on controlled descent, full depth, breathing pattern each rep. The volume week differs from W1 (moderate 6 reps) and W2 (heavy 4 reps) — DUP rotation ensures all intensity zones are hit.
Volume day push — higher reps, lighter load. 3 × 10. Approximately 10–15 kg on the sleeve. Focus on full range of motion and shoulder stability. Superset with barbell row (group A).
Week 3 — add 1 rep vs Week 2 (10 reps). Same weight as Week 2 or up to 2.5 kg more. Approximately 37.5–50 kg. Pair with landmine press (group A).
Week 3 progression: 13 reps vs 12 in Weeks 1–2, same weight — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Pair with hip thrust (group B).
Week 3 progression: 13 reps vs 12 in Week 2 at same weight — approximately 35–45 kg. Accessory volume for glutes. Pair with reverse lunge (group B).
Week 3 progression: 13 reps per side vs 12 in Weeks 1–2. Same resistance. Hold extended position for 2–3 s each rep.

metcon.name: Engine Builder
metcon.description: 16-minute EMOM — 4 movements rotating every minute. Minute 1: KB swing. Minute 2: push-up. Minute 3: air bike. Minute 4: lunge. Repeat 4 full rotations. Scale reps so you finish each minute with 10–15 s rest.

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Minute 1. Hip-snap drive. Only lumbar movement this metcon. Consistent bell height (shoulder).

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Minute 2. Strict or elevated. Maintain body plank. Scale reps to ensure full range.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Minute 3. Hard effort for 20 s then settle. Use arms and legs.

movements.name: lunge
movements.notes: Minute 4. Alternating. Bodyweight. Controlled descent. No bouncing.

mobility.name: Child's Pose with Lat Reach
mobility.notes: Lat stretch — frequency important

mobility.name: Thoracic Rotation (Quadruped)
mobility.notes: Different stimulus from roller work — active mobility

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent IR work

mobility.name: Quad and Hip Flexor Stretch (kneeling)
mobility.notes: After squat-dominant session

sessions.label: Workout 9 for the week starting July 12, 2026

warmup.name: Ski Erg Easy Pace
warmup.notes: Lat and upper back activation through the pull

warmup.name: Prone Thoracic Extension (Superman hold)
warmup.notes: Lie face down, raise chest and arms, hold. Erector and thoracic activation.

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (lying on side)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff warm-up

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Scapular Pull-Up Dead Hang
warmup.notes: Week 3 — aim for more control and range vs Weeks 1–2

warmup.name: Single-Leg Balance Eyes Closed
warmup.notes: Balance/proprioception mandatory element — progress to eyes closed if comfortable

strength.coachingNotes: Heavy day for the RDL — add 5 kg vs Session 6 Week 2 moderate day. Suggested: 52.5–62.5 kg. 4 × 5 reps. This is the highest load on the RDL this cycle. Control every inch of the descent — the hamstring stretch at bottom is the key driver for bone density loading. Full rest between sets.

Heavy week for pull-ups — use thinner band or attempt unassisted if possible. 4 × 5 strict reps. Rest 150 s between supersets. Superset with chest-supported row (group A). Pull-up at near-max effort — initiate movement from shoulder blades, elbows follow.
Week 3 — add 2 kg per DB vs Session 5 Week 2. Approximately 18–22 kg per DB. 3 × 8. Pair with pull-up (group A). Elbows at 45° off the bench, squeeze shoulder blades at top.

Week 3 progression: 13 reps vs 12 in Weeks 1–2, same weight — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Slow, controlled step-down (3 s eccentric) — builds bone density via eccentric loading. Pair with farmer carry (group B).
Week 3 — add 2 kg per hand if Week 2 felt manageable. Approximately 18–22 kg per hand. Mandatory loaded carry. Tall posture, brace core. Pair with step-up (group B).
Week 3 progression: 40 s vs 35 s in Week 2. If position degrades at 30 s, bend knees to 90° and continue. Quality over duration.

metcon.name: Row & Step Machine
metcon.description: 4 Rounds For Time. Target 12–14 minutes. Push-pull balance: ring rows balance the DB floor press. Rotate from ski erg (Session 7) to row machine today. Step-ups are the low-impact plyometric element.

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: No added load. Emphasis on power — drive up quickly (concentric) and lower slowly (eccentric 2 s). Low-impact plyometric element.

movements.name: ring row
movements.notes: Horizontal pull. Adjust feet to maintain challenging angle (more horizontal = harder).

movements.name: DB floor press
movements.notes: Horizontal push. Floor limits range — shoulder-safe. Lower until triceps touch floor.

movements.name: row machine
movements.notes: 15 calories per round. Strong leg drive.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Maintain throughout cycle

mobility.name: Book Opening (Side-Lying Thoracic Rotation)
mobility.notes: More reps than earlier sessions — improving range

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Extend hold by 5 s vs Week 2

mobility.name: Calf Stretch (wall)
mobility.notes: After step-up work — straight knee (gastrocnemius) and bent knee (soleus)

sessions.label: Workout 10 for the week starting July 19, 2026
sessions.focus: Lower Body Posterior Chain — Heavy Day (DUP: 3–5 reps)

warmup.name: Rowing Machine Moderate Pace
warmup.notes: Build to 65–70% effort over 4 minutes; prime the posterior chain

warmup.name: Hip 90/90 with Active Lift-Off
warmup.notes: In 90/90, lift the front shin off the floor for 3 s. Activates external rotators.

warmup.name: Thoracic Extension (hands behind head, seated)
warmup.notes: Sit on bench, hands behind head, extend upper back over the backrest

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Band External Rotation (elbow at side)
warmup.notes: Rotator cuff warm-up

warmup.name: Deadlift Warm-Up (bar → progressive)
warmup.reps: 5 at 20 kg, 4 at 40 kg, 3 at 55 kg
warmup.notes: Build to near working weight. Last set should feel fast and confident before the work sets.

strength.coachingNotes: Heavy week — add 2.5–5 kg vs Session 1 Week 1 (the previous heavy deadlift day). Suggested: 57.5–70 kg. This is the heaviest deadlift of the 4-week cycle. RPE 8. All reps must be crisp — if bar speed slows significantly on rep 3, stop the set and rest. Full 240 s between sets.
Week 4 — add 2.5 kg vs Week 3. Approximately 45–55 kg. Moderate-heavy for the hip thrust. Superset with lat pulldown (group A).
Week 4 heavy pull — increase weight by 2.5–5 kg vs Week 3 and drop to 6 reps. Approximately 32.5–42.5 kg. Heavy lat pulldown to match the heavy deadlift week. Pair with hip thrust (group A).
Week 4 (Weeks 3–4 add 1 rep rule): 13 reps at same weight as Weeks 1–2 — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Technique is the priority in Week 4; load stays the same. Pair with single-arm row (group B).
Week 4: 13 reps at same weight as Weeks 1–2 — approximately 14–18 kg DB. Pair with split squat (group B).
Week 4: maintain 11 reps per side from Week 3. Focus on slower tempo — 4 s lowering phase. Quality over quantity as we finish the cycle.

metcon.name: Tabata Finale A
metcon.description: Tabata protocol — 8 rounds of each movement (20 s work / 10 s rest), 1 min rest between movements. 4 movements × 4 min = 16 min total. Log total reps for each movement and compare to Week 2 Tabata (Session 5) to see improvement.

movements.name: air bike
movements.notes: Max calories per interval. Compare total across 8 rounds to Session 5 results.

movements.name: push-up
movements.notes: Maintain consistent reps each round. Full range.

movements.name: sit-up
movements.notes: Full range, hands touch mat overhead then touch toes.

movements.name: KB deadlift
movements.notes: Touch-and-go. Hip hinge, flat back. Lumbar-flagged — only one per this metcon.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Every session — must become a daily habit

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller
mobility.notes: Week 4 — extend duration. Breathe into the extension. Three thoracic segments.

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: IR maintenance

mobility.name: Figure-4 Hip Stretch
mobility.notes: After heavy posterior chain work

sessions.label: Workout 11 for the week starting July 19, 2026

warmup.name: Air Bike Moderate Pace
warmup.notes: Build to 65% effort by minute 4

warmup.name: World's Greatest Stretch
warmup.notes: Full hip, thoracic, and lat opener — comprehensive warm-up for squat day

warmup.name: Band Y/T/W Raises
warmup.notes: Scapular activation — warm-up only

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Back Squat Warm-Up (build to working weight)
warmup.reps: 5 at 20 kg, 5 at 35 kg, 3 at 45 kg
warmup.notes: Progressive build; each set faster than the last to prime neural drive.

warmup.name: Wrist Mobility — Prayer Stretch and Reverse Prayer
warmup.notes: Mandatory wrist/forearm element. Prayer: palms together in front of chest, push elbows apart. Reverse: backs of hands together, push down.

strength.coachingNotes: Moderate day — Week 4 adds a 5th set vs the 4 sets in Week 1 moderate. Add 2.5 kg vs Session 2 Week 1 (moderate squat day). Suggested: 47.5–57.5 kg. 5 × 6 is the highest volume-intensity combination of the cycle. RPE 7–8 on final set. Controlled 3 s descent.
Week 4 — add 2.5 kg vs Session 5 Week 2 (heavy press day), or match Session 2 Week 1 weight and aim for cleaner 8 reps. Approximately 12.5–17.5 kg on sleeve. Pair with barbell row (group A).
Week 4 — add 2.5 kg vs Session 8 Week 3. Approximately 40–52.5 kg. 3 × 10. Pair with landmine press (group A). Controlled throughout — no jerking.
Week 4: 13 reps same weight as Weeks 1–2 — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Accessory progression complete — set up for a load increase in the next cycle. Pair with hip thrust (group B).
Week 4: 13 reps same weight as Week 3 — approximately 35–45 kg. Accessory glute volume. Pair with reverse lunge (group B).
Week 4: maintain 13 reps from Week 3. Try to add 5% resistance (slightly thicker band or extra plate on cable) while keeping the same reps. Anti-rotation core — placed last.

metcon.name: Machine & Swing Complex
metcon.description: 14-minute AMRAP. Final push week — aim for more rounds than any previous AMRAP session. KB swings are the metcon staple of this program; track how your technique has improved over 4 weeks. Pull-ups balance the push (step-up). Ski erg rotates from air bike (Session 10).

movements.name: KB swing
movements.notes: Consistent swing — hip snap, bell to shoulder height. Lumbar-flagged — only lumbar movement here.

movements.name: pull-up
movements.notes: Strict or banded. Initiate with shoulder blades. Scale: ring row if needed.

movements.name: step-up
movements.notes: Drive up powerfully. Low-impact plyometric element for the cycle.

movements.name: ski erg
movements.notes: 10 calories per round. All upper body pull — complements the swing and pull-up.

mobility.name: Child's Pose with Lat Reach
mobility.notes: Week 4 — try to reach further; notice improvement vs Week 1

mobility.name: Thoracic Rotation (Book Opening)
mobility.notes: Active thoracic mobility — the movement should feel easier than Week 1

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Consistent IR work — you should notice increased range vs Week 1

mobility.name: Hip Flexor Kneeling Stretch
mobility.notes: Post-squat standard. Squeeze rear glute to deepen stretch.

sessions.label: Workout 12 for the week starting July 19, 2026

warmup.name: Ski Erg Easy-Moderate Pace
warmup.notes: Final week — activate lats with intention; notice the connection from the pull through to the hips

warmup.name: Prone Y/T/W on Bench (with light plates)
warmup.notes: Progress from bodyweight: hold 1–2 kg plates per hand. Rotator cuff — warm-up only.

warmup.name: Band Pull-Apart
warmup.notes: Warm-up only

warmup.name: Scapular Pull-Up Dead Hang
warmup.notes: Full dead hang, shrug scapulae down and together. Note any improvement in shoulder position vs Week 1.

warmup.name: Single-Leg Balance with Arm Reach
warmup.notes: Balance on one leg, reach opposite arm in various directions (forward, diagonal, lateral). Progressive balance/proprioception element for final week.

warmup.name: Rice Bucket Drill or Reverse Wrist Curls
warmup.notes: Mandatory wrist/forearm element — final session of the cycle.

strength.coachingNotes: Volume day for final week — add 2.5 kg vs Session 3 Week 1 volume RDL. Suggested: 47.5–57.5 kg. 4 × 10. This is the most accumulated volume for the RDL in this cycle. Smooth, controlled reps — no bouncing. The 4th set should challenge you but not break form. Record your weight for the next cycle's starting point.
Volume day — 3 × 10 pull-ups. Use band if needed. If you've been progressing the band size, you may now be on a lighter band than Week 1. Note improvement. Pair with chest-supported row (group A).
Week 4 — add 2 kg per DB vs Session 9 Week 3. Approximately 20–24 kg per DB. 3 × 10. Pair with pull-up (group A). Final set should be RPE 8.
Week 4: 13 reps same weight as Weeks 1–2 — approximately 8–12 kg per DB. Document your weight for the next cycle where you will start at a heavier load. Pair with farmer carry (group B).

Week 4 — extend the carry distance from 30 m to 35 m at same weight. Approximately 18–22 kg per hand. This is the final loaded carry of the cycle — note how it compares to Week 1. Pair with step-up (group B).

Week 4: maintain 40 s from Week 3. If 40 s is comfortable with perfect form, add 5 s as a bonus. Log your final hollow hold time — it will be the starting point for the next cycle. Core placed last as always.

metcon.name: Cycle Closer
metcon.description: For Time — 3 rounds. This is your benchmark finisher for the 4-week cycle. Record your time and compare total volume to Week 1 (Session 2 and 3 metcons). Goblet squat load can be increased if the 16 kg felt easy in earlier weeks. Med ball slams are the mandatory low-impact plyometric — finish strong.

movements.name: goblet squat
movements.notes: Increase to 20 kg KB if 16 kg is comfortable. Full depth, upright torso.

movements.name: single-arm row
movements.notes: Slightly heavier DB than Week 3 metcon if strength has increased. Full range each rep.

movements.name: med ball slam
movements.notes: Higher reps than Week 3 (10 → 12). Explosive — low-impact plyometric element. Step back to pick up.

movements.name: burpee (no-jump)
movements.notes: Step feet, no jump. Lumbar-flagged — only one lumbar movement in this metcon alongside the med ball slam requires note: med ball slam IS lumbar-flagged. SUBSTITUTION: replace burpee with 10 push-ups to comply with the 1 lumbar movement limit. Use push-up variant instead — chest to floor, no jump at top.

mobility.name: Doorway Lat Stretch
mobility.notes: Final session — extended duration. Compare your range to Week 1. Celebrate any improvement.

mobility.name: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller (full thoracic)
mobility.notes: Work all three thoracic segments. By Week 4, this should feel more comfortable and produce greater range.

mobility.name: Sleeper Stretch
mobility.notes: Final IR session — hold slightly longer. Record any subjective improvement in shoulder freedom.

mobility.name: Supine Spinal Twist
mobility.notes: Lie on back, draw one knee across the body. Gentle rotation to decompress after 4 weeks of loading. A perfect end to the cycle.
